canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, text classification is overwhelmingly defined as the task of assigning a discrete, predefined label to an input text sequence. This general framing encompasses a wide variety of specific tasks, including topic classification, sentiment analysis, stance detection, and question classification. A smaller body of work extends this to more specialized domains, such as classifying financial texts (e.g., 'hawkish-dovish classification'), identifying data practices in privacy policies, or predicting occupations from biographies. The field operationalizes and measures this behavior using a diverse set of benchmark datasets, with the most frequently cited instruments being AG News, TREC, SST-2, IMDb, DBPedia, and the GLUE benchmark suite. Other datasets such as Subj, Financial Phrasebank, and SIB-200 are also used, though less commonly in this set of papers. Text classification serves as a common downstream task for evaluating models under various learning paradigms, including in-context learning, zero-shot prompting, and continual learning. Beyond being an evaluation task in itself, it is also used to measure other constructs like model sensitivity, faithfulness, and social bias, with specific tasks like 'Resume classification' being employed to probe for allocational harms. The task is also studied in relation to synthetic data generation, which one paper reports can improve classification performance.

definitions.text: The task of identifying the viewpoint or stance of an author within a text, such as 'affirmative' or 'negative'.

definitions.text: A pre-training task involving data from multiple modalities (e.g., images and text) where parts of the input are masked and the model must predict the masked content.
definitions.paper: Multi-Head Mixture-of-Experts

definitions.text: Assigning a text instance to toxic or non-toxic categories, or to a finer-grained toxicity-related class set.
definitions.paper: Soft-Label Integration for Robust Toxicity Classification

definitions.text: Classifying financial headlines according to whether they contain specific market-related information.
definitions.paper: FinBen: A Holistic Financial Benchmark for Large Language Models

definitions.text: Classifying monetary-policy text by whether it signals tightening, easing, or neutrality.
definitions.paper: FinBen: A Holistic Financial Benchmark for Large Language Models

definitions.text: The task of categorizing sentences in financial texts, such as earnings calls, into argumentative functions like 'claim' or 'premise'.
definitions.paper: FinBen: A Holistic Financial Benchmark for Large Language Models

definitions.text: Classifying merger-and-acquisition mentions as completed deals or rumors.
definitions.paper: FinBen: A Holistic Financial Benchmark for Large Language Models

definitions.text: The task of detecting and classifying Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) related concerns within financial documents.
definitions.paper: FinBen: A Holistic Financial Benchmark for Large Language Models

definitions.text: Judging whether a knowledge graph triplet is valid or correct.
definitions.paper: MKGL: Mastery of a Three-Word Language

definitions.text: The task of classifying a single sentence into a predefined category, such as determining its grammatical acceptability or sentiment.
definitions.paper: Maintaining Structural Integrity in Parameter Spaces for Parameter Efficient Fine-tuning

definitions.text: The task of assigning a label to a text input, especially in scenarios where visual data is converted into descriptive text for evaluation.
definitions.paper: Large Language Models are Interpretable Learners

definitions.text: Assigning a natural language inference label to sentences across multiple languages.
definitions.paper: MrT5: Dynamic Token Merging for Efficient Byte-level Language Models

definitions.text: Assigning a comment to a stance category such as favor or against with respect to a posed question.
definitions.paper: The Power of LLM-Generated Synthetic Data for Stance Detection in Online Political Discussions

definitions.text: The observable task of classifying segments of a privacy policy into predefined categories related to data collection, use, and sharing.
definitions.paper: Empowering Users in Digital Privacy Management through Interactive LLM-Based Agents

definitions.text: The observable task of identifying the persuasion techniques used in media content such as advertisements.
definitions.paper: Teaching Human Behavior Improves Content Understanding Abilities Of VLMs

definitions.text: The task of assigning a news article to one of several predefined categories based on the content of its headline.
definitions.paper: Teaching LLMs How to Learn with Contextual Fine-Tuning

definitions.text: The task of assigning a predefined category or label to a given sentence, such as for sentiment or topic analysis.
definitions.paper: Second-Order Fine-Tuning without Pain for LLMs: A Hessian Informed Zeroth-Order Optimizer

definitions.text: The observable task of assigning a categorical label to an entire input sequence.
definitions.paper: ZETA: Leveraging $Z$-order Curves for Efficient Top-$k$ Attention

definitions.text: The task of classifying a pair of texts, for example, determining if they are paraphrases or have a specific relationship.
definitions.paper: Your Mixture-of-Experts LLM Is Secretly an Embedding Model for Free

definitions.text: Categorizing social media notes into predefined topics or taxonomies based on content, in single- or multiple-choice format.
definitions.paper: SNS-Bench: Defining, Building, and Assessing Capabilities of Large Language Models in Social Networking Services

definitions.text: Mapping a given document to a predefined topic cluster based on its content, using document embeddings and clustering techniques.
definitions.paper: Synthesizing Privacy-Preserving Text Data via Finetuning *without* Finetuning Billion-Scale LLMs

definitions.text: Determining whether a resume is appropriate for a given job category, used to assess allocational bias in hiring-related decisions.
definitions.paper: Position: Rethinking LLM Bias Probing Using Lessons from the Social Sciences

definitions.text: Predicting a person's job based on biographical information, used to assess whether models rely on gender or other stereotypes.
definitions.paper: Position: Rethinking LLM Bias Probing Using Lessons from the Social Sciences

definitions.text: The task of classifying a question into a category based on the type of answer it expects (e.g., person, location, numeric).
definitions.paper: A Systematic Analysis of Base Model Choice for Reward Modeling

definitions.text: The observable task of classifying a given question as either 'evergreen' (having a stable answer over time) or 'mutable' (having an answer that changes).
definitions.paper: Your Language Model Can Secretly Write Like Humans: Contrastive Paraphrase Attacks onLLM-Generated Text Detectors

definitions.text: Assigning labels to lengthy documents based on their content.
definitions.paper: CARD: Cross-modal Agent Framework for Generative and Editable Residential Design

definitions.text: Categorizing questions into types such as count, comparative, or multihop based on their structure and reasoning requirements.
definitions.paper: VELA: AnLLM-Hybrid-as-a-Judge Approach for Evaluating Long Image Captions

definitions.text: The task of assigning a label to each sentence in a text that indicates its role or category, such as a section header.
definitions.paper: Textual Aesthetics in Large Language Models

definitions.text: The process of assigning section labels (e.g., Background, Results) to sentences in a plain language summary based on content and context.
definitions.paper: Textual Aesthetics in Large Language Models

definitions.text: Determining the type of graph problem (e.g., TSP, Vertex Cover) from natural language descriptions and extracting constraints and optimization objectives.
definitions.paper: Multi-Document Event Extraction Using Large and Small Language Models

definitions.text: The task of assigning a predefined category or label to a scientific paper based on its content.
definitions.paper: GRASP: Replace Redundant Layers with Adaptive Singular Parameters for Efficient Model Compression

definitions.text: The task of assigning one or more labels to an input, such as a sentence, from a predefined set of categories.
definitions.paper: MixLoRA-DSI: Dynamically Expandable Mixture-of-LoRAExperts for Rehearsal-Free Generative Retrieval over Dynamic Corpora

definitions.text: The observable task of assigning zero, one, or multiple labels from a predefined set to a single text example, reflecting the co-occurrence of moral dimensions.

(Section 4.1)

relationships.paper: Reimagining Safety Alignment with An Image
relationships.evidence: We evaluate our models on 9 text classification benchmark datasets to ensure a comprehensive assessment across multiple domains, text lengths, and classification types - sentiment analysis, movie reviews, news categorization, and social media analysis. These are: AG News (Zhang et al., 2015), Emotion (Saravia et al., 2018), Stanford Sentiment Treebank (SST2 & SST5) (Socher et al., 2013), Multiclass Sentiment Analysis, Twitter Financial News Sentiment, IMDb (Maas et al., 2011), and Hate Speech Offensive (Davidson et al., 2017).

emnlp25: Reimagining Safety Alignment with An Image

characterization: Across the surveyed literature, text classification is overwhelmingly defined as the task of assigning a discrete, predefined label to an input text sequence. This general framing encompasses a wide variety of specific tasks, including topic classification, sentiment analysis, stance detection, and question classification. A smaller body of work extends this to more specialized domains, such as classifying financial texts (e.g., 'hawkish-dovish classification'), identifying data practices in privacy policies, or predicting occupations from biographies. The field operationalizes and measures this behavior using a diverse set of benchmark datasets, with the most frequently cited instruments being AG News, TREC, SST-2, IMDb, DBPedia, and the GLUE benchmark suite. Other datasets such as Subj, Financial Phrasebank, and SIB-200 are also used, though less commonly in this set of papers. Text classification serves as a common downstream task for evaluating models under various learning paradigms, including in-context learning, zero-shot prompting, and continual learning. Beyond being an evaluation task in itself, it is also used to measure other constructs like model sensitivity, faithfulness, and social bias, with specific tasks like 'Resume classification' being employed to probe for allocational harms. The task is also studied in relation to synthetic data generation, which one paper reports can improve classification performance.
